# Figure description: скв_166.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_166.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite well/borehole log (геологический разрез скважины / каротажная диаграмма)** — a multi-track geophysical well log combined with a lithological/stratigraphic column and associated data tables. It appears to be a standard Soviet-era комплексный каротаж (complex wireline log) format, likely including multiple geophysical curves plotted against depth, accompanied by core description columns, formation boundaries, and summary data tables in the header and right-hand margin.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single deep borehole (скважина)**, most likely one of the parametric or structural drilling holes associated with the regional survey of map sheets **M-42 and/or N-42** in northern Kazakhstan. The log appears to span a **substantial depth interval** — based on the vertical extent of the figure and typical Soviet regional borehole scales, likely on the order of **500–2000+ metres**. The figure includes:

- A **header block** (верхняя таблица) at the top containing borehole identification data, geographic/administrative location, survey parameters, and drilling specifications
- Multiple **log tracks** running vertically through the central portion
- A **lithological column** on the left side
- **Stratigraphic interval markers** and formation boundary annotations
- A **right-hand data table** (сводная таблица / каротажная сводка) with interval-by-interval petrophysical or core data
- A **lower section** that appears to show a **summary or correlation curve** at a compressed scale

The borehole number is not clearly legible at this resolution, but given the report context, it may be one of the numbered структурно-параметрических скважин (structural-parametric boreholes) referenced in Report 36572.

---

## Key Content

**Log Tracks (central panel):**
The central portion of the figure shows **two or three geophysical curve tracks** plotted against depth:

- The **leftmost active curve track** shows a high-amplitude, irregular waveform with large lateral deflections — consistent with a **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** log or possibly **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / spontaneous potential)** curve. The curve shows pronounced high-amplitude excursions in the upper-middle portion of the log, suggesting **resistive lithologies** (possibly carbonates, evaporites, or cemented sandstones) interbedded with lower-resistivity intervals.

- A **second curve track** to the right of the first appears smoother in some intervals and more irregular in others — consistent with a **гамма-каротаж (ГК / gamma ray)** or **нейтронный каротаж (НГК / neutron log)**, used to distinguish clay-rich from clean lithologies.

- There is a visually distinct **change in log character** at several depth horizons, marked by horizontal lines across the tracks — these represent **stratigraphic or formation boundaries (литолого-стратиграфические границы)**.

**Notable Features:**
- The **upper portion** of the log (roughly the first quarter of depth) shows relatively subdued curve response, suggesting a more uniform lithology (possibly Mesozoic or Paleogene cover sediments).
- The **middle portion** shows the most dramatic curve excursions — large, sharp resistivity highs — possibly corresponding to **Paleozoic carbonate or volcanic basement** intervals of interest for mineral exploration.
- The **lower portion** shows a return to moderate but variable amplitude response.
- Near the **base of the log**, the curve character changes again, possibly marking entry into **crystalline basement** or a different tectonic block.

**Lithological Column (left margin):**
A narrow column with standard Soviet lithological symbols (hatching, dots, brickwork patterns) represents the interpreted lithology. Visible patterns suggest alternating sequences of:
- Argillites / shales (горизонтальная штриховка)
- Sandstones / siltstones (точечный рисунок)
- Possibly carbonates or volcanics (brick or irregular patterns) at depth

**Right-hand Table:**
A detailed tabular summary occupies the right ~25% of the figure width, organized in rows corresponding to depth intervals. Columns likely include: interval depths (from–to), thickness, lithological description codes, core recovery percentage (выход керна), and possibly porosity or other petrophysical parameters.

**Bottom Summary Panel:**
The lowermost section shows a **compressed-scale correlation log** or **summary curve**, possibly a lithological profile at 1:2000 or 1:5000 scale for regional correlation purposes.

Due to the low resolution and faded quality of this Soviet-era document, full transcription is not possible, but the following elements are discernible or inferable:

**Header (top):**
- Likely contains: **Буровой журнал / Каротажная диаграмма** (borehole log / wireline log diagram)
- Fields for: **Скважина №** ___, **Площадь** (area name), **Организация** (organization — likely Казгеология or a subordinate expedition)

- Date fields and surveyor signatures

**Axis Labels (depth track):**
- Depth markers at regular intervals — likely every **50 or 100 metres**
- Possibly range from ~**0 to 1500–2000 м**

**Curve Track Headers:**
- Likely labeled: **КС** (or **КМ, КВ**), **ПС**, **ГК**, or similar standard Soviet wireline log abbreviations
- Scale values for resistivity possibly: **0–100 Ом·м** or logarithmic

**Stratigraphic Labels (left margin):**
- Era/period abbreviations in Cyrillic: possibly **Mz** (мезозой), **Pz** (палеозой), **K** (мел / Cretaceous), **J** (юра / Jurassic), **P** (пермь / Permian), **C** (карбон / Carboniferous), **D** (девон / Devonian)
